threads.subject: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - Sql Server License Verification

llm_summary.one_line: SQL Server license compliance issue: clarify if Aptum polices customer license violations.
llm_summary.summary: Barbara raised concern that Aptum could be liable/fined if Microsoft audits a customer's SQL Server license usage. Adam looped in Fred Taylor-Young to assess if this is an 'abuse' issue. Fred clarified that Abuse historically handled service misuse (bitcoin mining, illegal sites), not software license violations. He questions whether Aptum should police customer license compliance at all, noting risk of liability if Aptum misinterprets agreements. Adam needs to decide on policy and next steps.
my_actions: Decide whether Aptum will police customer software license compliance or treat it as customer responsibility
Clarify policy with legal/leadership if needed regarding liability for customer license violations
Provide direction to team on how to handle this ticket and future similar cases
tracked_actions: Barbara/team awaiting guidance on how to proceed with ticket 12028
Fred/InfoSec need clarity on whether license issues fall under their remit
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Thanks Fred. I agree this shouldn't fall under Abuse. We need to clarify our policy here — I'll check with legal on our liability exposure for customer license violations. My initial take is we notify the customer of potential compliance issues but don't police their licenses. I'll get back to the team with clear guidance by end of week so we can close ticket 12028 appropriately.
